Utricularia verrucosa, a species of bladderwort, is a carnivorous plant that obtains essential nutrients by capturing small organisms in its modified leaves called bladder traps. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. Utricularia spp. contain bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Utricularia spp. include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
